tasks.title: How do we have cross app pub sub
tasks.description:
Investigate and come up with a plan the MCP way

## Solution: Daemon Pub/Sub via Unix Socket IPC

The daemon already supports cross-app pub/sub through its channel subscription system:

### Architecture
```
App A (MCP Client) ──┐
                     ├──► Daemon (Unix Socket) ──► Pub/Sub Channels
App B (Beam UI) ─────┘
```

### How It Works
1. **Subscribe**: Apps connect via `subscribeChannel(photonName, channel, handler)`
2. **Publish**: Apps publish via `publishToChannel(photonName, channel, message)`
3. **Wildcard**: Supports `prefix:*` patterns (e.g., `kanban:*` matches all kanban events)

### MCP Integration
- Daemon tools exposed as `beam/daemon/subscribe`, `beam/daemon/publish`
- SSE transport streams channel messages to connected clients
- Dev server subscribes to daemon for reload notifications

### Implementation Files
- `src/daemon/client.ts` - Client functions (subscribe, publish, etc.)
- `src/daemon/server.ts` - Server handles channel routing
- `src/daemon/protocol.ts` - Message types

### Hot-Reload Integration (just implemented)
When photon code changes:
1. Dev server calls `reloadDaemon(photonName, photonPath)`
2. Daemon reloads code, updates sessions, preserves subscriptions
3. Publishes `system:{photonName}` event to notify subscribers

comments.author: ai
comments.content: Completed investigation. The daemon already has cross-app pub/sub via Unix socket IPC. Also implemented daemon hot-reload (commit 66b447b) which uses this system to notify subscribers when photon code changes.

tasks.title: debug text in kanban html
tasks.description:
Real-time kanban UI updates when MCP calls modify data

## Completed
- [x] Verified kanban emits board-update events
- [x] Verified UI listens for photon:board-update
- [x] Found MCP handler wasn't forwarding emits (direct method call bypassed execution context)
- [x] Added broadcast parameter to createBeamMCPSession
- [x] Added loader parameter to use executeTool with proper context
- [x] Created outputHandler to capture and forward board-update emits
- [x] Fixed PHOTON_NAME not set in cli.ts mcp command
- [x] Fixed hot reload to ignore data files (.json, boards/*)
- [x] Fixed photon-core daemon broker to extract photon name from channel

## Architecture
Two paths for real-time updates:
1. **In-process** (Beam MCP WebSocket): MCP call → outputHandler → broadcast → browser
2. **Cross-process** (daemon pub/sub): MCP call → emit → daemon → Beam subscribe → browser

## Pending
- [x] Publish photon-core with daemon broker fix
- [x] Update photon to use new photon-core
- [x] Publish photon package
- [x] Restart MCP process to pick up changes

## Debug text
The original issue (debug text in kanban HTML) was fixed in commit cea346c.

tasks.title: Hash-based channel IDs for daemon pub/sub
tasks.description:
## Summary
Replace readable channel names with hash-based IDs for multi-tenant isolation.

## Current
`kanban:photon` - readable but collides in multi-tenant scenarios

## Proposed
Hash the full identifier string to get unique channel ID:
- Input: `kanban:/Users/arul/.photon/kanban/boards/photon.json`
- Output: `a7f3b2c9d1e4` (hash)

## Implementation
- [ ] Create hash function for channel ID generation
- [ ] Update PhotonMCP base to compute channel ID from data path
- [ ] Set channel ID during photon initialization
- [ ] Update emit() to use hashed channel ID
- [ ] Update daemon broker to work with opaque IDs
- [ ] Test multi-tenant isolation (different data dirs → different channels)

## Benefits
- Multi-tenant safe
- Same data path = same channel (guaranteed)
- Different installations = isolated
- Simple opaque identifier on the wire

tasks.title: Fix kanban real-time updates not reflecting
tasks.description:
## Bug Report
Tasks created/moved via MCP don't reflect in the UI until MCP is refreshed.

- [x] Investigate why board-update events aren't reaching the UI
- [x] Check if subscription is active for the board
- [x] Verify SSE connection is receiving events
- [x] Test fix with live browser debugging

## Root Cause
**Not a bug** - The MCP server used by Claude Code was running stale code. When the MCP connection was refreshed (`/mcp` command), it reconnected and real-time updates work correctly.

## Verified Working
- Created task via MCP → UI updated immediately
- Backlog count changed from 5→6 without page refresh
- SSE connection active and receiving events
tasks.context: Tested with Chrome DevTools - real-time updates work when Beam server and MCP are in sync. Issue was stale MCP process.

comments.author: ai
comments.content:
**Additional fix: mtime-based cache invalidation (2026-01-26)**

Found another real-time sync issue: The kanban photon was caching board data without checking file mtime. When Claude Code's MCP modified the JSON file, the Beam server returned stale cached data.

**Fix applied:**
- Added `boardMtimes` Map to track file modification times
- `loadBoard()` now checks if file mtime changed before using cache
- `saveBoard()` updates mtime after write

**Commit:** e30111d in photons repo
